item.title: Ashish in the wild
item.dc:creator: Ashish
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe now

What? Why?

Hellu Hellu, I’m Ashish, I’m a 26 years old living in Bangalore. “Damn 26?!” I know right? Is that enough for an intro? Let’s just jump to what then, what is this? This is sort of my journal for this year. I wanted to create a space where I could yap about anything and everything. I want to write down what I felt like writing down - my passing thoughts, the way I perceive things, things I wish and plan to do, basically anything my stupid brain deems to be important. Why though? because I feel I need to, I have to and I want to. I feel it doesn’t exist and I want it to exist.

Where it all started

I went to Mumbai for the New Year on almost a solo trip, and I think that’s how it started, that’s when I realized I have this need that I want to express. I wanted to write my thoughts; I wanted to draw a little something with that writing to remember. I left Bangalore in a hurry, very dramatically caught my flight by a few minutes. I remember when I landed in Mumbai, it was 6 in the morning, the air felt different. I was scared and nervous, a hundred thoughts going through my mind but as soon as I sat in the cab, I noticed a song which felt familiar, suno na suno na sunlo na it was comforting and I just enjoyed it. I haven’t experienced this before in Bangalore, that’s how I knew I was in Bombay.

Word of the year

Every year me and my friends we decide a word of the year, and we try to incorporate that in our lives, however way we want it to. This year I have got two words - Patience and Discipline, I feel anything is achievable with these. By the year’s end I want to be financially responsible, really fit and healthy and ready with my master’s prep. I also want to write and draw more often, read a lot, go solo exploring parts of the city, read the history and create animations out of it just the way I perceive it. So yes, a lot.

Last thing, how often?

I want to write and post every two to three days. I’ll try my best to be consistent with it but since it’s my first time doing something like this, I’d like to keep a lot of room for growth so let’s figure it out as we move forward. Also, if you feel there’s something I can improve/work upon, literally anything, please don’t hold yourself back from giving suggestions.

Thank you for reading my substack! :)

item.dc:creator: Ashish
